basics.name: Alex Chen
basics.headline: Software Engineer | ML Systems | Full Stack
basics.summary: Computer science student with experience building full-stack web applications and machine learning systems. Comfortable across the stack from React frontends to Python backends to cloud deployment.

bullets: Built a real-time alert aggregation service in Python that reduced on-call noise by 40%.
Migrated three internal dashboards from AngularJS to React, cutting load time by 60%.
Wrote an ETL pipeline using Airflow and BigQuery to process 2M+ device events per day.
Collaborated with firmware and data platform teams in a two-week Agile sprint cycle.
Trained and evaluated object detection models (YOLOv8) on custom drone footage datasets.
Built a data labeling pipeline using Label Studio that cut annotation time by 50%.
Reproduced three prior baselines and documented failure modes for a UAM safety paper.
Presented findings at the 2025 UCSC Research Symposium.

projects.name: AutoShake
projects.description: End-to-end pipeline that generates tailored resume PDFs from Handshake job postings using LLMs.
bullets: Built a FastAPI backend that runs a two-stage LLM pipeline (skill extraction → resume rewrite).
Integrated Claude API with adaptive thinking for high-quality resume tailoring.
Designed a WeasyPrint-based PDF generation layer from a structured JSON schema.
Built a Chrome extension that scrapes job postings and sends them to the backend.

projects.name: Distributed Key-Value Store
projects.description: A fault-tolerant distributed KV store implementing the Raft consensus algorithm.
bullets: Implemented leader election, log replication, and snapshotting in Go.
Achieved linearizable reads and writes under network partitions in a 5-node cluster.
Written as part of MIT 6.824 distributed systems coursework.

projects.name: Campus Event Finder
projects.description: Full-stack web app that aggregates UCSC campus events from multiple sources.
bullets: Built REST API with Node.js and PostgreSQL, deployed on Railway.
Scraped and normalized event data from five campus sources using Cheerio.
Developed React frontend with real-time search and filter UI.
